title: 6. Partitioning — split work and keys on purpose
description: Logical keys today; physical shards later. Same key shape everywhere.
story:
Partitioning means: do not treat the whole world as one pile.

• Data partition: only this tenant / product line's knowledge.
• Work partition: ETL one product_ids slice at a time.
• Key partition: cache and rate-limit keys include tenant|product so customers never share a bucket.

Today this repo mostly uses LOGICAL keys (strings). Physical Neo4j Fabric shards are a later scale step — same keys, different storage.
one_liner: Same key language for cache, rate limit, and future shards: tenant|product|…
why_it_matters: Prevents one noisy tenant from exhausting everyone's rate limit.
Keeps ETL batches small and reviewable.
Prepares for multi-region without renaming everything later.
say_aloud: A partition key names the slice of the world we are working on.
Rate limits and caches must use the same key dimensions.
Selection-scoped promote is work partitioning by product_id.
Logical keys now; physical Fabric or multi-DB later if needed.

cheat_sheet.meaning: Key design only — one DB still

beats.title: Canonical key shape
beats.narrative: Boring strings prevent subtle multi-tenant bugs.
beats.say_after: tenant always; product when the data is product-scoped.

line_quiz.prompt: Why product in subgraph cache key?
line_quiz.why: Otherwise wm data could be served for esp.

pro_tips: TRICK: partition by a key (tenant/product) to spread load; always scope queries by that key (Kleppmann, DDIA Ch.6).
GOTCHA: watch for hot keys/skew — one huge tenant can unbalance an otherwise even partition scheme.

concepts.definition: Process a subset (product_ids) per pipeline run.

self_quiz.q: Why same key shape for cache and rate limit?

common_mistakes: Global rate limit key for all tenants.
Cache key missing tenant in multi-tenant SaaS.
final_boss: Give one logical and one physical partition example.
